metadata.notes: RE-ASSESSED after PR #1171: codex now has an HTTP hook channel (POST /api/v1/hooks/codex). PermissionRequest → HandlePermissionHook → PermissionPending → state_classifier rule 0 → waiting; Stop → ready. LIVE-CONFIRMED under codex 0.144.4. daemon_capability: full (was incapable). Recording DEFERRED: the installed hook POSTs to hardcoded http://localhost:7837, but the :7838 coexist recorder never receives it — record on a :7837 --record daemon via `of record run --attach`.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes (codex `-a/--ask-for-approval untrusted` gates every non-trusted command on a blocking approval overlay)
daemon_capability: **full** (was `incapable`) — PR #1171 gave codex an HTTP hook channel; a held approval overlay now drives a real `working → waiting` transition. **LIVE-CONFIRMED.**
driver_capability: ready (the codex interactive driver has `send`, `wait_turn`, `keys`, `sleep` — every step type the recipe needs)

## Why the prior `incapable` verdict is now STALE

The committed metadata judged `daemon=incapable` on the thesis that codex's Source is `FilesUnderRoot` with **no HTTP hook channel of the Claude-Code kind**, so `state_classifier.go` rule 0 (`PermissionPending → waiting`) is **dead for codex** and `PermissionPending` is always false. That thesis is now FALSE. PR #1171 (epic #1129) added exactly that hook channel:

1. **`core/adapters/inbound/agents/codex/hooks.go`** — a `POST /api/v1/hooks/codex` receiver. `PermissionRequest` and `PostToolUse` dispatch to `HandlePermissionHook`; `Stop` dispatches to `HandleStopHook` (carrying the turn's final assistant text). Session id is resolved from the transcript path, not the payload's shared `session_id`.
2. **`core/adapters/inbound/agents/codex/hookinstaller.go`** — installs curl `type: command` hooks into `~/.codex/hooks.json` (a dedicated file, never `config.toml`). PermissionRequest/PostToolUse carry matcher `".*"`; Stop takes no matcher. Version-gated to codex ≥ 0.114.0 (`codexSupportsHooks`), sentinel-idempotent, atomic write.
3. **`core/adapters/inbound/agents/codex/agent.go`** — a new `hooks` permission (`permission.KindModify`) whose `Apply` closure runs `applyCodexHooks()` (install) and `Remove` runs `UninstallHooks()`.
4. **`core/cmd/irrlichd/startup.go:917`** — `registerHookRoutes` wires `POST /api/v1/hooks/codex` → `codex.NewHookHandler(detector, permService, logger)`.

Net effect: `PermissionRequest → HandlePermissionHook` (`session_detector_lifecycle.go:282`) → `state.Metrics.PermissionPending = true` (`session_detector_activity.go:867`) → `state_classifier.go:35` rule 0 → **waiting**. `Stop → HandleStopHook` (`session_detector_lifecycle.go:347`) → turn-end → **ready**. `PostToolUse` clears the pending overlay once an approved tool runs. Rule 0 is no longer dead for codex.

## LIVE VERIFICATION (promotes daemon from doc/binary-strings to live-confirmed)

Under codex-cli **0.144.4** with a worktree-built `irrlichd` on `:7837` (`IRRLICHT_PERMISSION_MODE=grant-all`), codex was driven under tmux with `-a untrusted -s read-only` on a write task. At the approval prompt (a TUI overlay, NOT written to the rollout JSONL) the daemon flipped the codex session to **waiting**; after approving and the turn ending it went **ready**. The `hooks` permission auto-installed `~/.codex/hooks.json` (PermissionRequest & PostToolUse matcher `".*"`; Stop no matcher). This is the exact arc the scenario asserts, observed on a live session — the prior assessment's provisional emission claim is now settled.

## Scenario signal

`tool-gate-permission-prompt` asserts `ready → working → waiting` when a tool invocation opens a permission prompt (the load-bearing episode), with the recipe capturing entry into waiting and stopping. The hook path above produces exactly this. The resolve half (`waiting → working → ready`) and the late-hook no-bounce are also covered by the live arc, but — mirroring the claudecode sibling — the spec stops at the waiting entry.

## Recording is DEFERRED (infra limit, NOT a capability limit)

The installed hook POSTs to a **hardcoded** `http://localhost:7837` (`hookinstaller.go` `hookEndpointURL`). The factory coexist recorder runs on `:7838` and `tools/onboarding-factory/scripts/run-cell-multi.sh` explicitly notes that codex/claudecode hooks (hardcoded to :7837) reach **production, not the :7838 recorder**. So a coexist recording on :7838 would never receive the PermissionRequest hook and could not capture the waiting transition. This is the SAME hardcoded-port limitation claudecode's hooks carry — and claudecode's own tool-gate cell was recorded by attaching to a :7837 `--record` daemon. So the daemon is CAPABLE now; the recording is deferred to a `:7837 --record` daemon via `of record run --attach` (or a future configurable hook port). Route stays **record** (pending-record) — the honest "recordable in principle, not yet recorded" state — never `frozen`, which is reserved for a daemon that fundamentally cannot observe.

## Deployment detail to record

codex gates hook EXECUTION behind a one-time interactive "Trust hooks" prompt: after irrlicht installs the entries into `~/.codex/hooks.json`, the user must trust them in codex before they fire. A recording run must complete that trust step first.
caveats: daemon=full is LIVE-CONFIRMED (codex 0.144.4, worktree irrlichd on :7837): a `-a untrusted -s read-only` write task flipped the session to waiting at the approval overlay, then ready after approval. This supersedes the prior doc+binary-strings `incapable` verdict, which is now stale (PR #1171 added the hook channel it assumed absent).

RECORDING DEFERRED by a hardcoded-port infra limit, not a capability gap: the installed hook POSTs to hardcoded http://localhost:7837 (hookinstaller.go hookEndpointURL). The :7838 coexist recorder (run-cell-multi.sh) never receives it, so a coexist recording cannot capture the waiting transition. Record instead against a :7837 --record daemon via `of record run --attach` (same path claudecode's tool-gate cell used), or add a configurable hook port. of validate stays green: this is a pending-record cell, and validate does not demand a recording for a record-route cell.

Deployment prerequisite: codex gates hook EXECUTION behind a one-time interactive "Trust hooks" prompt. After irrlicht installs entries into ~/.codex/hooks.json the user must trust them in codex before any hook fires — a recording run must complete that trust step.

Recipe launch flag: the codex process must be launched with `-a untrusted -s read-only` so a write task escalates to the approval overlay. The interactive driver currently boots `codex --no-alt-screen` with no approval flags, so recording this cell needs the driver's boot line to carry those flags (the live verification launched them by hand under tmux). This is a launch-configuration precondition, not a missing step type — every script step type (send/wait_turn/keys/sleep) is present, so driver=ready.

PostToolUse (not PostToolUseFailure — codex has none) is the sole pending-clear signal; a denied approval that aborts the turn with no following PostToolUse clears the overlay only on the next tool call (known gap, issue #1174). The recipe holds in waiting and never approves, so this gap is out of the asserted arc.

sources.note: NEW (PR #1171): POST /api/v1/hooks/codex receiver. PermissionRequest/PostToolUse → HandlePermissionHook; Stop → HandleStopHook. Codex now HAS the HTTP hook channel the prior assessment said was absent.

NEW (PR #1171): installs curl hooks into ~/.codex/hooks.json, version-gated ≥ codex 0.114.0, sentinel-idempotent. hookEndpointURL is HARDCODED http://localhost:7837 — the source of the recording deferral on the :7838 coexist recorder.
NEW (PR #1171): `hooks` permission (permission.KindModify) with Apply=applyCodexHooks / Remove=UninstallHooks install closures, alongside the existing transcripts observe permission.
registerHookRoutes (line 917) wires POST /api/v1/hooks/codex → codex.NewHookHandler(detector, permService, logger) — the receiver is live in the daemon.
Rule 0 (line 35): PermissionPending → waiting, the most authoritative gate. Now reachable for codex via the hook path — the prior 'rule 0 dead for codex' reasoning is obsolete.

HandlePermissionHook (line 282) sets PermissionPending; HandleStopHook (line 347) handles turn-end. Codex's HookTarget is *services.SessionDetector — the same agent-agnostic surface claudecode's hooks use.

Lines 23-24: the isolated daemon is NOT on :7837, so codex/claudecode hooks (hardcoded :7837) reach production, not the :7838 recorder — the concrete reason a coexist recording cannot capture the hook-driven waiting transition.

Sibling reference: same scenario, hook-driven waiting via rule 0, daemon=full, recorded against a :7837 --record daemon — the template for codex's now-symmetric verdict and for the :7837 recording path.
Codex presession-birth template: proc-<PID> presession born ready, rollout session reconciles with a distinct session_id, pid_bind, then working/waiting phases anchored to the REAL session.

preconditions: codex CLI ≥ 0.114.0 (hooks support; live-confirmed on 0.144.4) on PATH and authenticated (~/.codex/config.toml or OPENAI_API_KEY)

irrlichd is recording on :7837 via a --record daemon (of record run --attach) — NOT the :7838 coexist recorder: the installed hook POSTs to hardcoded http://localhost:7837, so :7838 never receives PermissionRequest
irrlicht's codex `hooks` permission is granted so applyCodexHooks() installs PermissionRequest/PostToolUse/Stop into ~/.codex/hooks.json
the user has completed codex's one-time interactive 'Trust hooks' prompt so the installed hooks actually fire
setup: The interactive driver launches codex in a fresh per-run cwd under <staging>/cwd/ with `-a untrusted -s read-only --no-alt-screen`, accepts the trust dialog, and waits for the TUI banner + MCP boot before sending the first prompt

Under `-a untrusted -s read-only`, any model-proposed write/command escalates to a blocking approval overlay; the recipe never answers it, so the session holds in waiting until teardown (mirrors the claudecode sibling that stops at waiting)

script.text: Create a new file called hello.txt in the current directory containing the text 'hi' using a shell command. Do it now.

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created (proc-<PID>) event in ready within 1000 ms of agent launch
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new for the rollout session with a distinct session_id, born ready
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered event bound to the rollout session_id
events.jsonl contains a state_transition→working on the rollout session_id when the prompt is sent
events.jsonl contains a state_transition→waiting on the SAME rollout session_id once the PermissionRequest hook fires (PermissionPending=true → rule 0), while the approval overlay is held
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition→ready on the rollout session_id after the waiting transition (the recipe never approves; it terminates in waiting)
